rationale: The only change was employer names swapped from specific real companies (re:cinq, Optilyz, GISCollective) to generic placeholder-style names (Acme, Pinepoint, Northwind Labs). The 1-point score drop tracks with sharper but legitimate reasoning about the actual job stack — the variant calls out missing PostgreSQL/Nest.js experience and overqualification, none of which keys off the employer names. Both evaluations share the same core concerns (overqualified, basic German, niche DLang focus), and no concern in the variant references the renamed employers or treats them as less rigorous/credible. The differences read as ordinary run-to-run rewording and noise rather than a penalty tied to the demographic change.
